Edge findings (non-blocking, ghi chú để fix sau)
Manual UI smoke (chờ demo qua browser)
Feature 2 — HRM core: Department + Employee + OrgChart
Feature 3 — Attendance: WorkSchedule + Device + Log + Timesheet UI
entries.summary: Plan tính năng HRM, **sau** khi xong [refactor.md](refactor.md) (Phase 1+2+3 + Phase 4 docs/MCP unification — root scaffolding, identity, docs gộp ra root, infra Docker chạy, Prisma migrate `_init` áp dụng).

entries.title: C-HR refactor plan
entries.description: 3-phase refactor để gộp 2 boilerplate ban đầu thành 1 monorepo C-HR có doc/MCP thống nhất.
headings: Phase 1 — Root scaffolding (combine 2 boilerplate ra ngoài)
entries.summary: **Project**: C-HR (C-OpenAI Human Resource) — SaaS quản lý nhân sự cho doanh nghiệp. **Stack**: Next.js 16 (frontend) + NestJS 10 (backend) + Postgres 16 + Redis 7, monorepo.

entries.title: Pending verification — F1 + F2 runtime checks
entries.description: Items shipped without runtime verification yet. Walk through these in a browser + Prisma Studio after `./scripts/dev.sh start:dev` + migrate + seed.
entries.summary: Backlog of things shipped through commits since 2026-05-04 that have **only** been verified at compile time (`pnpm build` / `pnpm typecheck`). Each entry gives the smallest set of clicks / curl calls that prove the path works end-to-end.

entries.title: ZK-Bridge — pull-side adapter for ZKTeco / Hikvision attendance devices
entries.description: Sống trong monorepo (services/zk-bridge), nhưng *deploy tách rời* tại LAN văn phòng khách hàng. Polls device qua TCP/SDK, dịch sang generic JSON contract, push vào C-HR backend ở cloud.
headings: Tại sao trong monorepo nhưng deploy tách rời
Defer cho phiên bản tiếp theo
entries.summary: > **Trạng thái**: Defer sang sau khi F1–F5 đóng + có khách hàng thật cần tích hợp device. Trước đó **không** code. > > **Vị trí**: `services/zk-bridge/` (monorepo) nhưng deploy **độc lập** tại văn phòng khách hàng — không chạy cùng C-HR backend.

entries.title: ADR 0001: <decision title>
entries.description: Template for project architecture decision records.
entries.summary: > **Template** — copy to a new file (e.g. `0002-use-graphql.md`) and replace.

entries.title: ADR 0001: Tenant isolation via manual repository pattern
entries.description: Mỗi truy vấn data của Org tự thêm filter organizationId trong repository per-service, không dùng Prisma extension auto-filter.
entries.summary: - **Status**: Accepted - **Date**: 2026-05-03 - **Deciders**: @nguyendinhphongdx

entries.title: ADR 0002: Audit log via @Auditable interceptor
entries.description: Cấu trúc audit_logs, scope các action cần ghi, retention.
entries.summary: - **Status**: Accepted - **Date**: 2026-05-03 - **Deciders**: @nguyendinhphongdx

entries.title: ADR 0003: No permission engine — code if/else + AppAdmin model
entries.description: Không dùng RBAC decorator/permission table. Phân quyền viết if/else trong service, dựa UserType + AppAdmin record.
entries.summary: - **Status**: Accepted - **Date**: 2026-05-03 - **Deciders**: @nguyendinhphongdx

entries.title: ADR 0004: OrgChart source of truth — Department.managerId + parentId
entries.description: Department tree là single source. Manager chain query qua Postgres recursive CTE. Employee không có directManagerId.
headings: Logic suy ra "quản lý của X"
Truy vấn — Postgres recursive CTE (1 query, không N+1)
entries.summary: - **Status**: Accepted - **Date**: 2026-05-03 - **Deciders**: @nguyendinhphongdx

entries.title: ADR 0005: Backend folder structure — src/apps/<context>/<module>/
entries.description: Tách bounded contexts theo nghiệp vụ HR (core, hrm, attendance, requests, …) thay vì 1 thư mục modules/ phẳng.
entries.summary: - **Status**: Accepted - **Date**: 2026-05-03 - **Deciders**: @nguyendinhphongdx

entries.title: ADR 0006: Universal Request engine — RequestGroup + Request + side-effects
entries.description: One polymorphic table for every kind of request (leave, attendance correction, OT, OOO, …) instead of per-type tables. Validated by JSON fieldsSchema, dispatched on approve via a code-keyed registry.
entries.summary: - **Status**: Accepted (supersedes the per-type tables introduced in F4) - **Date**: 2026-05-04 - **Deciders**: @nguyendinhphongdx

entries.title: ADR 0007: Service reads from RequestContext, controllers stop threading currentUser
entries.description: Services consume the authenticated user via the AsyncLocalStorage RequestContextService instead of receiving a currentUser parameter from controllers. Repositories still take primitives explicitly. Background jobs / auth flow / CLI keep explicit params.
headings: Helper API — methods on ctx (`Org-level checks, sync`)
Helper API — standalone functions for **app admin** check (needs DB)
Conventions / lint rules to add later
entries.summary: - **Status**: Accepted - **Date**: 2026-05-04 - **Deciders**: @nguyendinhphongdx

entries.title: Add a CLI command
entries.description: How to register a new `pnpm cli <name>` command.
entries.summary: The CLI runner reuses the full DI graph via `CliModule`. Each command is an `@Injectable()` class implementing the `CliCommand` interface.

entries.title: Add a feature module
entries.description: Step-by-step recipe for creating a new NestJS feature module in this codebase.
entries.summary: Suppose you're adding a `post` module with CRUD over a `Post` resource.

entries.title: Auth & cookies
entries.description: How JWT + httpOnly cookies + Bearer fallback work, and how to extend.
entries.summary: ``` POST /auth/login ↳ AuthService.login() verifies credentials, signs accessToken + refreshToken ↳ Controller calls setAuthCookies(res, tokens, configService) - res.cookie('access_token', ..., { httpOnly, secure, sameSite, domain, maxAge }) - res.cookie('refresh_token', ..., { ... refresh maxAge }) ↳ Response also returns { user, accessToken, refreshToken } in body so non-cookie clients (mobile, server-to-server) can extract them. ```

entries.title: Storage providers
entries.description: Use local filesystem, S3, or GCS — the StorageProvider interface stays the same.
headings: Using the storage in a service
entries.summary: `StorageModule` chooses a provider based on `STORAGE_TYPE` env var (`local` | `s3` | `gcs`). Code consumes the abstract `StorageProvider` interface, so switching is a config change.

entries.title: Switch database engine
entries.description: Move between Postgres / MySQL / SQLite / SQL Server / MongoDB.
entries.summary: Prisma's `datasource.provider` cannot be templated via `env()` — it must be a literal. The boilerplate works around this with a small script:

entries.title: Environment variables
entries.description: Full reference of every env var the app reads, with defaults and notes.
entries.summary: All env vars are loaded by `@nestjs/config` from `.env.local` then `.env` (in that order). Inside services, **always** read via `ConfigService`, not `process.env` directly. See [conventions.md](../conventions.md#configuration).

entries.title: Global services
entries.description: Providers available everywhere via DI without module re-import.
headings: Using globals from outside the request scope
entries.summary: Anything in `libs/` is `@Global()` — once `AppModule` imports it, the providers are injectable from any feature module without re-importing.

entries.title: Architecture
entries.description: Layered structure, dependency injection, and HTTP request lifecycle.
entries.summary: The codebase has four layers, each depending only on layers above it:

entries.title: Conventions
entries.description: Naming, file organization, and concrete do/don't rules.
headings: DTO vs interface — when to use what
entries.summary: | Kind | Convention | Example | | --- | --- | --- | | File | kebab-case + role suffix | `user.service.ts`, `login.dto.ts`, `parse-uuid.pipe.ts` | | Class | PascalCase + role suffix | `UserService`, `LoginDto`, `ParseUUIDPipe` | | Interface | PascalCase, prefix `I` only for JWT-style payloads | `RequestUser`, `IJwtPayload` | | Constant | SCREAMING_SNAKE | `MAX_PAGE_SIZE` | | Enum | PascalCase, members SCREAMING_SNAKE | `UserRole.ADMIN` | | Method | camelCase, verb-first | `findById`, `issueTokens` | | Boolean | `is*` / `has*` / `should*` | `isPrivate`, `hasRequiredRole` | | Folder | kebab-case | `request-context/` |

entries.title: C-HR backend documentation
entries.description: NestJS-specific architecture, conventions, recipes, reference for the C-HR backend.
entries.summary: NestJS-specific patterns cho [apps/backend](../../apps/backend/) (layered architecture, DI, Prisma, auth flow, recipes). HRM business domain (entity, invariant) sống ở [../domain.md](../domain.md), không ở đây.

entries.title: Add a feature module
entries.description: Step-by-step recipe for adding a new feature under src/features/.
entries.summary: Suppose you're adding a `posts` feature with list/detail/create.

entries.title: Add a page (route)
entries.description: Step-by-step recipe for adding a new App Router page that delegates UI to a feature view.
entries.summary: Pages live under `src/app/`. The convention is **page = thin wrapper, view = the actual UI** — keeps routes hot-swappable.

entries.title: Auth flow
entries.description: How cookies + middleware + axios refresh interceptor + TanStack Query work together.
headings: Cross-site cookies (FE on different domain than BE)
entries.summary: ```text cookie present? browser ───────────► middleware.ts (edge) │ NO → redirect /login?next=… │ YES → continue ▼ RSC route renders │ ▼ client hydrates │ TanStack Query ▼ GET /auth/me ──► apiClient (axios, withCredentials) │ 401? │ └─► /auth/refresh (deduped) │ ok? retry original │ fail? redirect /login ▼ user data → useAuth() hook ```

entries.title: Icons
entries.description: When to use lucide-react vs custom inline SVG vs an icon library, and how to add a new icon.
entries.summary: | Need | Use | | --- | --- | | UI icon (arrow, check, eye, settings, …) | `lucide-react` (already installed) | | Brand mark (GitHub, Google, X, Discord, Apple, …) | Add a file in [`src/components/icons/`](../../../apps/frontend/src/components/icons/) | | 15+ brand marks at once | Install `simple-icons` (see "Scaling up") |

entries.title: Theming & design tokens
entries.description: How CSS variables, dark mode, animations, and the shadcn registry hang together.
entries.summary: All design tokens live in [src/app/globals.css](../../../apps/frontend/src/app/globals.css):

entries.title: Environment variables
entries.description: Every env var the app reads, what it does, and where it's consumed.
entries.summary: All `NEXT_PUBLIC_*` vars are inlined at build time. Server-only vars (no prefix) are only available during SSR / server actions / route handlers.

entries.title: UI tokens & utilities
entries.description: Animation classes, scrollbar utility, design tokens reference.
entries.summary: Defined in [src/app/globals.css](../../../apps/frontend/src/app/globals.css).

entries.title: Architecture
entries.description: App Router layers, RSC vs Client boundaries, auth flow, request lifecycle.
headings: RSC vs Client — the cardinal rule

entries.title: Conventions
entries.description: Naming, file organization, and concrete do/don't rules.
headings: DTO vs interface — when to use what
entries.summary: | Kind | Convention | Example | | --- | --- | --- | | File (component) | PascalCase | `LoginForm.tsx`, `BrandPanel.tsx` | | File (hook) | kebab-case + `use-` or camelCase `useFoo.ts` | `use-mobile.ts`, `useAuth.ts` | | File (route) | lowercase, kebab-case folder | `app/forgot-password/page.tsx` | | File (helper) | kebab-case + `.util.ts` / `.types.ts` | `pagination.util.ts`, `user.types.ts` | | Class | PascalCase | `Pagination`, `User` | | Component | PascalCase | `function LoginForm()` | | Hook | camelCase, prefix `use` | `function useAuth()` | | Constant | SCREAMING_SNAKE | `MAX_PAGE_SIZE`, `LAST_EMAIL_KEY` | | Boolean | `is*` / `has*` / `should*` | `isLoading`, `hasSession` | | Folder | kebab-case for routes, lowercase for features | `(auth)/`, `landing/`, `auth/` |
